MandateOutput","name":"output","type":"tuple"}],"name":"setAttestation","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"}],"devdoc":{"details":"**Fill Function Patterns:** This contract provides two distinct fill patterns with different semantics: 1. **Single Fill (`fill`)** - Idempotent Operation:    - Safe to call multiple times    - Returns existing fill record if already filled    - Suitable for retry mechanisms and concurrent filling attempts    - Use when you want graceful handling of already-filled outputs 2. **Batch Fill (`fillOrderOutputs`)** - Atomic Competition Operation:    - Implements solver competition semantics    - Reverts if first output already filled by different solver    - Ensures atomic all-or-nothing batch filling    - Use when you need to atomically claim an entire multi-output order **IMPORTANT SECURITY NOTE - FIRST SOLVER ORDER OWNERSHIP:** By default, the owner (address able to claim funds after and order is settled) is the solver of the first output, in case of an order with multiple outputs. This leads to some security considerations: For Users: 1. Denial of Service Risk: The solver of the first output may refuse to fill the other outputs, delaying the order execution until expiry (when user can be refunded).    - Mitigation: Users should ensure that the first output is the most important/valuable, making this attack more costly. 2. Exploitation of different order types: When opening orders, users are able to set different rules for filling them, i.e., output amounts could be determined by a dutch auction. If the order has multiple outputs, the solver of the first output (the owner) can delay the filling of other outputs, which could lead to worse prices for users.    - Mitigation: Users should be cautious when opening orders whose outputs have variable output amounts. In general, users SHOULD NOT open orders where any output other than the first has an order type whose output amount is determined by a time based mechanism. Note that other mechanisms can also be used to manipulate prices. Users should also consider opening orders with exclusivity for trusted solvers, especially when the order has multiple outputs. For Solvers: 1. Multiple outputs risk: When filling an order, the solver MUST be aware that they will only be able to finalise the order (i.e., claim funds) after filling all of the outputs (potentially in multiple chains). If the solver is unable to do so, the user will be refunded and the order will be considered as not filled.    - Mitigation: Solvers should be aware of all of the risks and variables, such as:      - all outputs must be filled before `fillDeadline` and the proof of the filling transaction must be handled by each oracle before `expiry` time.      - Solvers should be aware that some outputs have callbacks, which is an arbitrary code that is executed during the filling of the output. They should refuse orders with callbacks outside of the primary batch (that containing the first output) unless it's known that it can't possibly revert (considering that a successful off-chain simulation is not a guarantee of on-chain success)        They should understand the risks of each callback and the potential for them to revert the filling of the output, which could lead to the solver not being able to finalise the order.","errors":{"AlreadyFilled()":[{"details":"Attempting to fill an output that has already been filled by a different solver"}],"FailedCall()":[{"details":"A call to an address target failed. The target may have reverted."}],"FillDeadline()":[{"details":"Fill deadline has passed"}],"InsufficientBalance(uint256,uint256)":[{"details":"The ETH balance of the account is not enough to perform the operation."}],"InvalidAttestation(bytes32,bytes32)":[{"details":"Oracle attestation doesn't match stored fill record"}],"PayloadTooSmall()":[{"details":"Payload is too small to be a valid fill description"}],"SafeERC20FailedOperation(address)":[{"details":"An operation with an ERC-20 token failed."}]},"kind":"dev","methods":{"efficientRequireProven(bytes)":{"details":"More efficient implementation of isProven. If the output is already filled, it returns the existing fill record hash without reverting. This makes it suitable for retry mechanisms and scenarios where multiple parties might attempt to fill the same output.","params":{"fillerData":"The solver data containing the proposed solver.","orderId":"The unique identifier of the order.","output":"The `MandateOutput` struct to fill."},"returns":{"fillRecordHash":"The hash of the fill record."}},"fillOrderOutputs(bytes32,(bytes32,bytes32,uint256,bytes32,uint256,bytes32,bytes,bytes)[],uint48,bytes)":{"details":"This function implements atomic batch filling with solver competition semantics. Unlike the single `fill()` function, this is NOT idempotent - it will revert if the first output has already been filled by another solver. This ensures that only one solver can \"win\" the entire order. **Behavioral differences from single fill():** - REVERTS with `AlreadyFilled()` if the first output is already filled (solver competition) - Subsequent outputs can be already filled (they are skipped) - All fills in the batch succeed or the entire transaction reverts (atomicity) **Solver Selection Logic:** The first output determines which solver \"wins\" the entire order. This prevents solver conflicts and ensures consistent solver attribution across all outputs in a multi-output order.","params":{"fillerData":"The solver data containing the proposed f102e123aae6","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://239a4935f64046032c79397a7b213c9d4df06617f5bd4955fefd8d408d3a6e6e","dweb:/ipfs/QmTnwYKbyCi9qwCjUZSWpF8q9xwy9HS7nnoiA4WGeLLUju"]},"src/oracles/BaseInputOracle.sol":{"keccak256":"0xbf032de387df2308ed2ef6e34dad93e0dd4875ac5fc80a0d8b9b7ec0109243ab","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://1a05957977612735f6c7f6ee3b191160bdd0e8ebff936df8e02f0be8a0995c0e","dweb:/ipfs/QmUoAu2C6FjK5KQRakRt9UFtzvh9qfhnyEkGDRaHc56nzN"]},"src/output/OutputSettlerBase.sol":{"keccak256":"0x64343c2016bd229a08b8f7399345ddd1ea0ed71c8982c3cc375ae01c82192de5","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://38c74aff84d88294ec3f903a73fd1bd4d49774f7d746f2c8ca36fbb746c5c925","dweb:/ipfs/QmXRe9mjt96HGeucedtZG3KZoULAdwYbfTc4qbkzBrJuLF"]}},"version":1}

efficientRequireProven(bytes).details: More efficient implementation of isProven. Does not return a boolean, instead reverts if false. This function returns true if proofSeries is empty.

params.proofSeries: remoteChainId, remoteOracle, application, and dataHash encoded in chucks of 32*4=128 bytes.

fill(bytes32,(bytes32,bytes32,uint256,by.details: External fill interface for filling a single output (idempotent operation).This function is idempotent - it can be called multiple times safely. If the output is already filled, it returns the existing fill record hash without reverting. This makes it suitable for retry mechanisms and scenarios where multiple parties might attempt to fill the same output.

params.fillerData: The solver data containing the proposed solver.
params.orderId: The unique identifier of the order.

returns.fillRecordHash: The hash of the fill record.

fillOrderOutputs(bytes32,(bytes32,bytes3.details: This function implements atomic batch filling with solver competition semantics. Unlike the single `fill()` function, this is NOT idempotent - it will revert if the first output has already been filled by another solver. This ensures that only one solver can "win" the entire order. **Behavioral differences from single fill():** - REVERTS with `AlreadyFilled()` if the first output is already filled (solver competition) - Subsequent outputs can be already filled (they are skipped) - All fills in the batch succeed or the entire transaction reverts (atomicity) **Solver Selection Logic:** The first output determines which solver "wins" the entire order. This prevents solver conflicts and ensures consistent solver attribution across all outputs in a multi-output order.

params.fillerData: The solver data containing the proposed solver.
params.orderId: The unique identifier of the order.

getFillRecord(bytes32,(bytes32,bytes32,u.details: Retrieves the fill record for a specific order output by MandateOutput struct.

params.orderId: The unique identifier of the order.

returns.payloadHash: The fill record hash if the output has been filled, zero otherwise.

getFillRecord(bytes32,bytes32).details: Retrieves the fill record for a specific order output by hash.

params.orderId: The unique identifier of the order.
params.outputHash: The hash of the output to check.

returns.payloadHash: The fill record hash if the output has been filled, zero otherwise.

params.application: Application that the data originated from.
params.remoteOracle: Attestor on the data origin chain.

returns._0: bool Whether the hashed data has been attested to.

params.orderId: The unique identifier of the order.
params.output: The MandateOutput struct that was filled.
params.solver: The address of the solver who filled the output.
params.timestamp: The timestamp when the fill occurred.

efficientRequireProven(bytes).notice: Check if a series of data has been attested to.

fillOrderOutputs(bytes32,(bytes32,bytes3.notice: Atomic batch fill interface for filling multiple outputs (non-idempotent operation).

hasAttested(bytes[]).notice: Returns whether a set of payloads have been approved by this contract.

isProven(uint256,bytes32,bytes32,bytes32.notice: Check if some data has been attested to on some chain.

setAttestation(bytes32,bytes32,uint32,(b.notice: Sets an attestation for a fill description to enable cross-chain validation.
